title: Delegate run checkpoint
description: Typed, schema-validated state written at each delegate phase boundary. One JSON document per run. Each phase appends its section, then validates the document (this schema + the referential cross-checks in checkpoint.mjs) before the next phase reads its load-bearing fields — branch, worktree path, issue number — from it. The checkpoint is the single source of truth for those fields, a resume point after interruption, and the audit trail the Report phase reads.

version.description: Checkpoint schema version. Bump only on a breaking shape change.

runId.description: Stable id for this delegation run — reuse the team name (delegate-<timestamp>) for multi-issue runs, or delegate-single-<N>-<timestamp> for the single-issue fast path.

scope.description: Phase 1 output — what this run resolved to delegate.

mode.description: How the issue set was resolved from $ARGUMENTS.

description.description: Human-readable scope summary shown in the Phase 3 plan (e.g. the milestone title + number + open-issue count).

milestone.description: Set for the current-milestone / milestone modes; null otherwise.

issues.description: Phase 1 output — every issue in scope. An empty scope means there is nothing to delegate; the run stops before writing a checkpoint.

body.description: Optional — the issue body, retained so the Execute phase can build agent prompts without a second fetch.

classification.description: Phase 2 output — one entry per issue. Every issue number in `issues` must appear exactly once; no entry may reference an unknown issue.

area.description: Module / subdirectory the issue touches — drives parallelization.

touchesRoot.description: Whether the issue edits root/shared files (forces serialization).

touchesShared.description: Whether the issue touches the shared-module bottleneck (forces serialization).

plan.description: Phase 3 output — the confirmed assignment plan. `confirmed` records that the user approved (or that the single-obvious-assignment fast path skipped the gate).

confirmedVia.description: Optional — how confirmation was obtained: `interactive` (a human approved at the Phase 3 gate), `batch-scope` (delegate.batchMode was on and explicit scope stood in for the gate — no human pause), or `single-obvious` (the single-obvious-assignment fast path skipped the gate). Records the confirmation provenance so a batch run is auditable after the fact. checkpoint.mjs enforces that it may only be set when `confirmed` is true.

mode.description: parallel → each assignment gets its own worktree; serial → runs in the main checkout.

branch.description: Branch per git-workflow conventions. Load-bearing — the Execute phase injects this verbatim and cross-checks it against what the agent actually pushed.

worktree.description: Absolute worktree path for parallel groups; null for serial groups and the single-issue fast path. checkpoint.mjs enforces: non-null iff the group mode is parallel.

board.description: Board Setup output (optional) — project-board sync metadata. `enabled: false` means board sync was disabled and downstream board updates are skipped.

execution.description: Phase 4 output — one entry per planned assignment. `number` must reference a planned assignment and `branch` must equal that assignment's branch (this cross-check is what catches a hallucinated branch name).

pr.description: PR URL or #number when status is done; null otherwise.

boardMoved.description: Whether the board item was advanced (In Review / left In Progress).

autoMergeArmed.description: Present only when the delegate.autoMerge option was on: whether `gh pr merge --auto` actually armed on this PR. checkpoint.mjs enforces that only a PR-bearing entry can be true — a PR left open-but-not-armed records false, and an entry with no PR can never be armed.

approval.description: Present only when the delegate.approveBeforePush gate was on: whether the human approved this agent's push at the pre-push gate. checkpoint.mjs enforces that a rejected push is status 'skipped' with a null pr — a rejection can never show a PR.

approvedBy.description: Who approved or rejected the push at the approval gate (recorded when delegate.approveBeforePush is on). Requires `approval` to be set.

report.description: Phase 5 output — final report inputs. The Report table itself is rendered from `execution`.

notes.description: Failures, skipped issues, or worktree-cleanup caveats to surface in the summary.
